tickets.title: US001 - Student Registration
tickets.description:
As a Student,
I want to register using my .edu email address,
so that only university members can access the marketplace.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Users must provide a valid .edu email address.
- Non-.edu email addresses are rejected.
- Users receive confirmation after successful registration.

tickets.title: US002 - Create Listing by Barcode Scan
tickets.description:
As a Student,
I want to scan a textbook barcode,
so that item information can be filled automatically.

Acceptance Criteria:
- The camera can scan a barcode.
- ISBN and title are automatically populated.
- Users can edit imported information before posting.

tickets.title: US003 - Create Auction Listing
tickets.description:
As a Student,
I want to create auction-based listings,
so that buyers can compete for my item.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Sellers can choose auction mode.
- Sellers can define auction duration.
- The highest bidder is identified when the auction ends.

tickets.title: US004 - Safe Exchange Zone Selection
tickets.description:
As a Student,
I want to select a Safe Exchange Zone before chatting,
so that item exchanges occur in secure locations.

Acceptance Criteria:
- A Safe Exchange Zone must be selected.
- Chat remains disabled until a zone is selected.
- Approved exchange zones are displayed to users.

tickets.title: US005 - Manage Safe Exchange Zones
tickets.description:
As a Campus Administrator,
I want to manage Safe Exchange Zones,
so that students always have access to approved meeting locations.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Administrators can add new zones.
- Administrators can edit existing zones.
- Administrators can remove inactive zones.
- Changes are visible to users immediately.

analysis.ambiguity1: "This needs to be exclusive.": The term "exclusive" is vague because it does not clearly define the eligibility requirements beyond restricting access. It is unclear whether exclusivity means only current students, alumni, faculty, or anyone with a .edu email address.
analysis.ambiguity2: "I want to be able to auction items, not just set a fixed price.": The auction requirement lacks important details such as bidding rules, auction duration, minimum bid increments, reserve prices, and how a winning bidder is selected.
analysis.conflictSolution:
Conflict:
The Student Union President wants the marketplace to collect a 2% transaction fee to support student clubs. However, the University Legal Team states that the university cannot process payments or handle money.

Proposed Solution:
The application should not process payments or collect transaction fees directly. Students should complete payments externally using cash or third-party payment services such as Venmo. If funding is needed for student clubs, alternative methods such as voluntary donations or separate university-approved fees should be considered outside the marketplace platform.
analysis.nfr1:
Security
Only users with verified .edu email addresses may create accounts and access the marketplace.
analysis.nfr2:
Safety
The system must require users to select an approved Safe Exchange Zone before initiating communication regarding a transaction.
analysis.nfr3:
Usability
The application should support barcode scanning to automatically populate textbook information and reduce manual data entry.
